channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sen. Josh Hawley Demands Zuckerberg Testify Over Allegations of Undermining U.S. National Security
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley calls on Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to testify over allegations that the company undermined U.S. national security, following testimony from a former Facebook employee.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple has few incentives to start making iPhones in U.S., despite Trump’s trade war with China
item.description: It would take several years and cost billions of dollars to build new plants in the U.S., and then confront Apple with economic forces that could triple the price of an iPhone, threatening to torpedo sales of its marquee product.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ancient jawbone from Taiwan belongs to a mysterious group of human ancestors, scientists say
item.description: Researchers say a fossil jawbone discovered in Taiwan belonged to an enigmatic group of early human ancestors.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yes, Nintendo Switch 2 Edition games will contain the upgrades on the cartridge, but it seems Breath of the Wild DLC will still need to be purchased separately
item.description: Nintendo has confirmed that Switch 2 Edition games will contain the full game and upgrades on the cartridge, but there's uncertainty surrounding DLC.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Rise of Video Game Adaptations: From Flops to Blockbusters
item.description: Hollywood's obsession with intellectual property has led to a surge in video game movie adaptations, with films like Minecraft and The Super Mario Bros. Movie breaking records at the box office.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti benchmark leak hints at performance boost over its predecessor – but it won’t matter if it doesn’t stay at retail price
item.description: A new benchmark leak suggests Nvidia's RTX 5060 Ti may be great for budget GPU gamers, but that may not matter due to the state of GPU prices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How thieves use new credit card numbers before you receive them
item.description: The CyberGuy provides tips to protect yourself from criminals who use various methods to make unauthorized transactions using your account information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The new PS Portal system update brings a slew of improvements, including the ability to capture gameplay in cloud sessions, a sort feature, and more
item.description: A new PlayStation Portal update arrives today, bringing a ton of new features aimed at improving the user experience while using Cloud Game Streaming Beta.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI fears irreparable harm from Musk, files countersuit
item.description: OpenAI has filed a countersuit against Elon Musk, accusing the billionaire of a sustained campaign to damage the company and urging a US federal court to block further actions it described as unlawful and disruptive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Drones are the future of cybercrime
item.description: The Ukrainian military is teaching the world how to creatively use drones for battlefield offense and defense. Ukraine has built a vast arsenal of drones customized for precision strikes, reconnaissance, and kamikaze missions. But this innovation also extends to cybersecurity offense and defense.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TBM Gains Momentum Amidst Rising IT Complexity
item.description: TBM Council sees a 9% increase in membership, with organizations embracing the practice to better align IT spending with business value amidst growing tech investment.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why neurodivergent perspectives are essential in AI development
item.description: It’s impossible to go a day without talking or hearing about AI — and what makes it powerful and potentially dangerous. We’re well past its hype phase and now operate in a world where the technology has become an even more permanent fixture in our lives.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump's China tariffs derail push for TikTok deal
item.description: President Trump’s new tariffs on China have thrown a wrench in efforts to negotiate a deal over TikTok’s divestment from its parent company ByteDance, as Washington and Beijing sink deeper into a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: SAP customers on Business Suite: New strategy, same old concerns
item.description: SAP in February dusted off its legacy on-prem Business Suite label and resurrected it for the cloud era under a new strategic banner “Business Unleashed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tesla is launching sales in Saudi Arabia, where the obstacles do not (yet) include Musk protests
item.description: Tesla starts selling cars in Saudi Arabia on Thursday, a country where on a 900-kilometre (559 mile) stretch of its main east-west highway linking the capital Riyadh and the holy city of Mecca there isn’t a single charging station.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Deepfake porn is a labor issue
item.description: Last month, First Lady Melania Trump used her first public remarks of President Trump’s second term to voice her support for the Take It Down Act, a bipartisan bill aimed at curbing deepfake “revenge porn.” She was joined by Elliston Berry, a Texas teenager who was a victim of deepfake porn.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Wohl, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: Travelers should prepare for extra scrutiny of devices when crossing borders, especially when entering the U.S. Experts say 100% privacy might be impossible but there things you can do to make it harder for someone to see your private data.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 US preorders over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nintendo said U.S. preorders of its Switch 2 console will not take place on April 9 as it assesses tariff implications.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5
